mechanism_narrative: Higher recent growth in real activity and retail sales signals stronger near-term corporate revenue expectations; when GDPC1 and RSAFS momentum is positive, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive through earnings-revision channels.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows RSAFS at 350775 and GDPC1 at 11096.2
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 or RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional base rate over a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above its recent range tightens discount rates and reduces equity duration value; when FEDFUNDS crosses upward through 4.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if upward 4.0% FEDFUNDS crossings produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or average triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid unemployment-rate increase signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk; when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is below the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production acceleration reflects stronger operating leverage for cyclical firms; when INDPRO growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth over the prior three months, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows INDPRO at 119.657 and CPIAUCSL at 194.1
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-minus-CPIAUCSL growth interaction has a nonpositive coefficient or Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening household formation, construction demand, and credit-sensitive spending; when HOUST falls more than 10% over six months, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if six-month HOUST declines greater than 10% are followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above the unconditional average in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration paired with a non-rising policy rate indicates falling real policy restraint and risk of future tightening; when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS is flat or down, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: 3-month CPIAUCSL growth is positive and 3-month FEDFUNDS change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows CPIAUCSL at 194.1 and FEDFUNDS at 3.04
failure_condition: Reject if this CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS interaction is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirms broad income growth without recessionary labor stress; when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise over three months, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows PAYEMS at 133537.0 and UNRATE at 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-nonrising condition fails to improve 5-day S&P 500 hit rate by at least 5 percentage points versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden monthly contraction in industrial production represents an adverse supply and production shock that lowers near-term earnings expectations; when INDPRO falls more than 0.5% month over month, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly INDPRO drops greater than 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.
